title: Minuartia laricifolia
taxonomy: Minuartia laricifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. It belongs to the genus Minuartia and is native to certain regions in the world. The classification of Minuartia laricifolia is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Caryophyllales, family Caryophyllaceae, genus Minuartia, species M. laricifolia. The species is also sometimes referred to as Arenaria laricifolia. The exact classification may vary depending on the taxonomic source.
uses: Minuartia laricifolia is a plant species with reported uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia. The plant is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
